text: Here, as in other passages, God is represented as speaking directly through his Angel, instead of merely entrusting him with a message.

Some think the text here is corrupt or deficient; as it stands, we must suppose that the two bulls (or perhaps, in the Hebrew text, bullocks) were used to drag away the stones in demolishing the altar.

The Hebrew text is commonly understood as meaning, ‘If anyone pleads in favour of Baal, let him be put to death before tomorrow’s light’; cf. Job 13.8. But the interpretation here given, which is that of the Latin version, seems to fit the context far better.
